results.title: Traffic stop consent search
results.prompt: A driver was stopped for a broken taillight. The officer questioned him about drugs, asked to search the car, and the driver said “sure” after being told he could be arrested if he refused. Officers found a handgun in the trunk. Analyze suppression issues and likely arguments.

results.title: Warrantless cell phone search incident to arrest
results.prompt: After arresting a suspect on a state warrant, federal agents searched his cell phone without a warrant and found texts about a firearm purchase. The phone was seized incident to arrest. Analyze suppression and any applicable exceptions.

results.title: Miranda custody at home
results.prompt: Agents executed a warrant at a home and questioned the homeowner in his kitchen for 45 minutes while two agents blocked the exits. He was not told he was free to leave. He confessed to mail fraud. Analyze Miranda custody and suppression arguments.

results.title: Knock-and-announce violation
results.prompt: Agents executed a search warrant for a suspected drug house and entered 2 seconds after knocking. Evidence was found in the living room. Analyze whether knock-and-announce violations support suppression in this case.

results.title: Probable cause for search warrant
results.prompt: A warrant affidavit relied on an informant who claimed a defendant stored cocaine in a garage. The informant had not been used before, and there was minimal corroboration. Agents found drugs. Analyze probable cause and suppression issues.

results.title: GPS tracking without warrant
results.prompt: Agents placed a GPS tracker on a defendant’s car without a warrant and monitored it for 21 days, leading to a traffic stop and seizure of contraband. Analyze Fourth Amendment issues and suppression arguments.

results.title: Border search of laptop
results.prompt: At an international airport, CBP officers searched a traveler’s laptop without suspicion and found evidence of export violations. Analyze the border search doctrine and suppression prospects.

results.title: Protective sweep after arrest
results.prompt: Agents arrested a suspect at his front door and conducted a protective sweep of the house, finding a rifle in a bedroom. They did not see any threats. Analyze the sweep and suppression issues.

results.title: Inventory search of impounded car
results.prompt: After a DUI arrest, police impounded the car and conducted an inventory search, discovering counterfeit IDs. The department policy was inconsistent. Analyze suppression issues.

results.title: Exigent entry for gunshots
results.prompt: Officers heard gunshots and entered an apartment without a warrant, finding a felon with a firearm. Analyze exigent circumstances and suppression arguments.

results.title: Coerced confession
results.prompt: A suspect was interrogated for 10 hours, denied food, and threatened with loss of custody unless he confessed. He signed a statement admitting fraud. Analyze voluntariness and suppression.

results.title: Sixth Amendment right to counsel after indictment
results.prompt: After indictment, agents questioned the defendant about the charged offense without counsel present. He made incriminating statements. Analyze Sixth Amendment issues and suppression.

results.title: Brady/Giglio nondisclosure
results.prompt: The government failed to disclose that its key cooperating witness received a non-prosecution agreement in another case. The defense learned this after trial. Analyze remedies and standards for relief.

results.title: Jencks Act timing
results.prompt: At trial, the government produced a witness’s prior statements on the morning of testimony, giving the defense 15 minutes to review. Analyze Jencks compliance and potential relief.

results.title: Speedy Trial Act exclusion
results.prompt: The defendant was indicted in January, trial began in November, and the government sought multiple continuances for “complexity.” The defense objected. Analyze Speedy Trial Act issues.

results.title: Vindictive prosecution
results.prompt: The defendant rejected a plea offer and later the government added a new charge carrying a mandatory minimum. Analyze whether vindictive prosecution arguments apply.

results.title: Selective prosecution
results.prompt: A defendant claims he was prosecuted for a firearms offense while similarly situated defendants of a different race were referred to state court. Analyze standards for selective prosecution and discovery.

results.title: Grand jury subpoena for privileged records
results.prompt: A grand jury subpoena sought an attorney’s billing records from a law firm, potentially revealing defense strategy. Analyze privilege and potential motions to quash.

results.title: Indictment fails to allege mens rea
results.prompt: An indictment for wire fraud tracked the statute but omitted the word “knowingly.” The defense moved to dismiss. Analyze sufficiency of the indictment.

results.title: Conspiracy and withdrawal
results.prompt: The defendant participated in a drug conspiracy but claims he withdrew six months before a major shipment. The government charges him for the later shipment. Analyze withdrawal and liability.

results.title: Aiding and abetting liability
results.prompt: The defendant drove a friend to a bank, unaware of a robbery plan, but waited in the car. The friend robbed the bank, and the defendant fled with him. Analyze aiding and abetting liability.

results.title: Entrapment claim
results.prompt: Undercover agents repeatedly pressured a financially struggling defendant to buy narcotics, offering large sums. The defendant eventually agreed. Analyze entrapment and predisposition.

results.title: Duress defense
results.prompt: A defendant transported drugs after receiving threats to harm his family. He did not report the threats to police. Analyze the duress defense and burdens.

results.title: Rule 404(b) evidence
results.prompt: The government seeks to admit prior fraud convictions to show intent in a new fraud case. The defense objects under Rules 404(b) and 403. Analyze admissibility.

results.title: Confrontation Clause and lab report
results.prompt: The prosecution introduced a lab report through a supervisor who did not conduct the tests. The analyst was unavailable. Analyze Confrontation Clause issues.

results.title: Hearsay co-conspirator statement
results.prompt: The government wants to admit a co-conspirator’s statement to an informant about drug deliveries. Analyze Rule 801(d)(2)(E) requirements.

results.title: Expert reliability under Daubert
results.prompt: The government offers a forensic expert using a new tool to match shell casings. Defense challenges reliability. Analyze Daubert/Rule 702 issues.

results.title: Rule 403 prejudicial photos
results.prompt: The government seeks to introduce graphic crime scene photos in a felon-in-possession case. Analyze Rule 403 balancing.

results.title: Suppression of statements after re-initiation
results.prompt: After invoking counsel, the defendant was re-approached by agents in jail and questioned about a different offense. He agreed to talk. Analyze Miranda/Edwards issues.

results.title: Search of digital device at probation office
results.prompt: A defendant on supervised release had his phone searched by probation without a warrant. The conditions allowed searches for contraband. Analyze Fourth Amendment issues.

results.title: Rule 11 plea colloquy error
results.prompt: At the plea hearing, the judge failed to advise the defendant of the mandatory minimum. He pled guilty and later moved to withdraw. Analyze Rule 11 error and remedy.

results.title: Ineffective assistance at sentencing
results.prompt: Defense counsel failed to object to a PSR enhancement for leadership role. The defendant received a higher guideline range. Analyze Strickland prejudice.

results.title: ACCA predicate dispute
results.prompt: The defendant faces an ACCA enhancement based on prior state burglary convictions. The defense argues the statute is broader than generic burglary. Analyze categorical approach issues.

results.title: 924(c) crime of violence
results.prompt: The indictment charges 18 U.S.C. § 924(c) with Hobbs Act robbery as the predicate. The defense seeks to dismiss the 924(c) count. Analyze crime of violence issues.

results.title: Venue challenge
results.prompt: The alleged wire fraud scheme involved emails sent from one district and received in another. The defendant was tried in the district of receipt. Analyze venue.

results.title: Statute of limitations
results.prompt: A tax offense occurred in 2017; the indictment was returned in 2024. The government argues tolling applies. Analyze statute of limitations issues.

results.title: Materiality in false statements
results.prompt: The defendant is charged with 18 U.S.C. § 1001. The statements were false but arguably immaterial to the agency’s decision. Analyze materiality and defenses.

results.title: Wiretap necessity
results.prompt: A Title III wiretap was authorized based on affidavits claiming normal techniques were inadequate. The defense challenges necessity. Analyze suppression arguments.

results.title: CIPA discovery restrictions
results.prompt: The government invoked CIPA to limit access to classified documents. Defense claims the restrictions impair the ability to present a defense. Analyze potential motions and standards.

results.title: Restitution scope
results.prompt: After a fraud conviction, the court ordered restitution to victims not listed in the indictment. Analyze restitution scope and challenges.

results.title: Forfeiture nexus
results.prompt: The government seeks forfeiture of a defendant’s house, claiming it facilitated drug trafficking. The defense argues the nexus is too attenuated. Analyze forfeiture standards.

results.title: Bail detention hearing
results.prompt: The government moved for pretrial detention citing danger to the community and risk of flight. The defendant has strong family ties and no prior convictions. Analyze detention factors and rebuttal.

results.title: Supervised release revocation
results.prompt: The defendant on supervised release tested positive for drugs twice and missed appointments. The court seeks revocation. Analyze standards and options.

results.title: Compassionate release
results.prompt: An inmate seeks compassionate release due to serious medical conditions. The government opposes. Analyze criteria and arguments.

results.title: Sentencing variance based on history
results.prompt: The PSR recommends a guideline range of 87–108 months. The defense seeks a downward variance based on childhood trauma and rehabilitation. Analyze variance arguments.

results.title: Obstruction enhancement
results.prompt: At sentencing, the government seeks an obstruction enhancement based on alleged false testimony at trial. Analyze standards and defenses.

results.title: Loss calculation in fraud
results.prompt: In a healthcare fraud case, the government’s loss estimate includes intended loss far above actual loss. The defense disputes the calculation. Analyze loss calculation issues.

results.title: Discovery of informant identity
results.prompt: The defense seeks disclosure of a confidential informant’s identity who witnessed the alleged drug transaction. The government claims safety concerns. Analyze disclosure standards.

results.title: Evidentiary sufficiency on appeal
results.prompt: After conviction for possession with intent to distribute, the defendant argues the evidence was insufficient because drugs were found in a shared apartment. Analyze sufficiency standards on appeal.

results.title: Appellate plain error review
results.prompt: At trial, defense counsel did not object to a jury instruction on an element of the offense. On appeal, the defendant challenges the instruction. Analyze plain error review.
